text.title: release-planning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for release-planning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Ship "one more thing" after feature freeze because it is "already built".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Late additions bypass the readiness matrix and are the most common source of post-release regressions.

failure-mode.description: Generate release notes from a manifest captured at T-3 days and ship without regenerating at T-1.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Late-landing PRs and last-minute cuts make T-3 manifests stale. Always regenerate from the source of truth (merged-PR labels) at deploy time.

failure-mode.description: Train-the-monster: monthly release trains accumulate too much scope; one feature slips and the whole train slips.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Large trains make pull-or-postpone tradeoffs painful at the last minute. Counter: smaller trains with feature flags decouple readiness from ship date.

failure-mode.description: Communication-plan inflation: PM builds a matrix of every audience × every channel × every revision for every release.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Most rows produce zero customer behavior change. Cut to channels where engagement can be measured; delete the rest.

failure-mode.description: Rollback theater: every plan documents "Rollback Plan: revert PR" without ever drilling it in staging.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: First real rollback fails and discredits the PM. Drill rollback in staging at least quarterly with the on-call who would execute it.

failure-mode.description: Release notes written at release start and edited manually through the cycle rather than generated from merged-PR labels at deploy time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Manual notes drift from reality as PRs land and cuts happen. Stale notes ship inaccurate and surface as customer trust incidents.

failure-mode.description: PM as deploy gate: PM treats the release plan as a veto over engineering deploy mechanics.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Engineering owns deploy mechanics. The PM owns external readiness. Conflating the two makes the PM the bottleneck and erodes cross-functional trust.

failure-mode.description: Hidden dependencies: a feature depends on a back-office migration or vendor contract renewal not tracked in the engineering tracker.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Dependencies not in the manifest look green but are actually red. Mandate a cross-team dependencies section with a named owner per row.

failure-mode.description: Breaking-change detection delegated entirely to LLM agents reading PR titles.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents miss implicit breaking changes (default value flipped, undocumented header now required). Breaking-change rows must be human-asserted.

failure-mode.description: Cycle time tracked only as "ship-date hit-rate" rather than median feature commit-to-live time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ship-date hit-rate hides the truth: teams ship smaller things slower over time. Cycle time trend is the leading indicator of release health.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated PR contents — the agent invents a feature in the notes that does not exist in any merged PR.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force every notes bullet to cite a merge SHA in an HTML comment; lint that the SHA exists in git log.

failure-mode.description: Auto-publishing release notes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Never auto-publish. The agent emits a draft to a PR or doc; a human merges. One auto-publish of an inaccurate note destroys more trust than a year of well-edited drafts builds.

failure-mode.description: Sycophantic marketing voice — LLMs default to "thrilled" and "delighted".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add an explicit anti-fluff clause to the prompt and a post-generation lint that rejects drafts containing those tokens.

failure-mode.description: Breaking-change blindness — agents extracting notes from PR labels miss the implicit breaking change (a default value flipped, an undocumented header now required).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require breaking-change rows to be human-asserted, not LLM-derived from PR titles.

failure-mode.description: Migration-script hallucinations — agents generate confident ALTER TABLE examples that drift from the actual migration.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pull migration content from the migration files in the PR, not from the agent's interpretation.

failure-mode.description: Stale manifest — an agent run at T-3 days that is not re-run at T-1 ships an obsolete manifest.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Make the manifest a CI artifact regenerated on every push to the release branch; consume the freshest only.

failure-mode.description: Comms-channel blast radius — agents posting drafts to a customer-visible Slack channel by mistake (typo in channel ID).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Restrict the agent's channel allowlist; require an explicit --channel arg, not a default.

failure-mode.description: Long-context manifest dumps — feeding the entire 200-row manifest into every prompt wastes tokens and dilutes attention.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Filter to customer_visible=Y for notes; filter to breaking_change=Y for migration guides. One filter per artifact.

failure-mode.description: Prompt injection via PR descriptions — a hostile or sloppy contributor pastes content into a PR description that the agent ingests verbatim into release notes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sanitize ingested text; never let agent-ingested PR text become customer-facing without human review.

failure-mode.description: Privacy bleed in retros — agents pulling support-ticket data into the post-release retro can inadvertently embed customer PII into a doc that goes wider than expected.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run password-scrubber-agent on every retro; redact ticket bodies, keep only counts and categories.

failure-mode.description: Versioning drift — agent picks v1.2.3 from a tag that was never pushed; or picks v1.2.4 while a hotfix already claimed v1.2.4.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always read tags from the upstream remote, never from local-only state; lint that the proposed version > all existing remote tags.

failure-mode.description: Cohort-flag mismatch — a release plan item names feature flag new-checkout but the deployed flag is checkout-v2.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Have the agent diff the manifest's flag-names column against the live flag-platform API; mismatch = hard fail.

failure-mode.description: Calendar-bound assumptions — "ship Tuesday" baked into prompts ages poorly across timezones, holidays, and frozen weeks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Encode the deploy window as a date range from a calendar source, not a string in the prompt.
